target.name: Cognition

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $400.0B.

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Seed-stage AI & Software company that has raised $175M+. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public: Palantir; 8 private), including same-stage and adjacent-stage frontier AI firms such as Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, Liquid AI, and Field AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using a $5M base value derived from total capital raised and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $12M. Peer-Median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $250M to $400B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in AI company valuations at frontier stages; Thinking Machines Lab commands $12B at Seed, while Sakana AI is valued at $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $10M (low) to $2.5B (high), with a median of $1.1B. Stage-Progression produces a materially lower implied value, suggesting either substantial step-up potential or that comparable multiples already embed execution risk. The primary uncertainty is whether Cognition's capital deployed and progress warrant positioning closer to top-quartile peers (Thinking Machines Lab) or mid-quartile (Sakana AI, Liquid AI). Specificity on product traction and revenue—unavailable here—would tighten this band considerably.
